hook.text: HealthSouth's CEO told employees: 'Make the numbers match Wall Street's expectations.'

buildup.text: For seven years, the company fabricated $2.7 billion in fake revenue to meet analyst targets.

discovery.text: Dozens of employees were pressured into making false entries. Refusal meant getting fired.

twist.text: CEO Richard Scrushy claimed he knew nothing. The jury acquitted him—a shocking verdict.

climax.text: He was later convicted of bribery in a separate case and sentenced to seven years.

punchline.text: Meeting Wall Street's number isn't worth prison time.

quiz.question: What drove HealthSouth's accounting fraud?
options: Pressure to meet Wall Street expectations
